text.title: javascript Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: `any` proliferation — return `any`, accept `any`, cast to `any` to silence errors.
antipattern.symptom: Production bugs that the type system should have caught; eslint warnings ignored at scale.
antipattern.root-cause: any is the easy escape hatch; defaults allow it.
antipattern.fix: Enable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any: error. Replace with unknown + narrowing or with proper types from zod.

antipattern.symptom: IDE rename misses callsites; tree-shaking less effective; grep -r "import default" yields chaos.
antipattern.root-cause: Habit from older CommonJS code; framework pages reinforce it.
antipattern.fix: Switch to named exports. Leave default only where framework requires (Next.js pages, dynamic imports).

antipattern.description: Treating fetched JSON as a typed object without runtime validation.
antipattern.symptom: Production crashes when API shape drifts; TS happily lets `data.user.email` blow up at runtime.
antipattern.root-cause: TS types disappear at runtime; trusting them across the network boundary is unsafe.
antipattern.fix: Wrap each external call in zod.parse() (or valibot). The parsed result carries the right type at runtime and compile time.

antipattern.description: Mocking fetch/axios directly in unit tests instead of MSW.
antipattern.symptom: Tests pass with mocked client but fail in Storybook / browser dev where the network is real; mock duplication across test files.
antipattern.root-cause: Tight coupling between tests and the HTTP client choice.
antipattern.fix: Define MSW handlers in src/test/handlers.ts; setupServer() in tests; same handlers reused in Storybook + browser dev.
